notes: Attempted to reproduce CS0809 'Obsolete member overrides non-obsolete member' warning for OnSizeChanged when inheriting from SKCanvasView. Inspected the compiled SkiaSharp.Views.Android.dll from the 2.80.2 NuGet package using System.Reflection.Metadata — OnSizeChanged has NO [Obsolete] attribute. The only [Obsolete] method in SKCanvasView is OnDraw(SKSurface, SKImageInfo). Source code at the v2.80.2 git tag confirms this. Built a .NET Android library inheriting from SKCanvasView and overriding OnSizeChanged with SkiaSharp.Views 3.116.1 and 3.119.1 — zero warnings. The asymmetric behavior (only CircularGauge affected, not SemiCircularGauge) and timing with the Xamarin.AndroidX upgrade strongly suggest the CS0809 warning originates from the reporter's project configuration or class hierarchy, not from SkiaSharp.

No [Obsolete] attribute found anywhere in the file.

versionResults.notes: Assembly metadata inspection of SkiaSharp.Views.Android.dll confirms OnSizeChanged has no [Obsolete] attribute. Cannot build a Xamarin.Android project (deprecated), but binary inspection is definitive.
.NET Android library inheriting from SKCanvasView and overriding OnSizeChanged builds with 0 warnings.
Source code inspection confirms no [Obsolete] attribute on OnSizeChanged.

actionability.reason: OnSizeChanged has never been marked [Obsolete] in any SkiaSharp version. The CS0809 warning is definitively not caused by SkiaSharp. The asymmetric behavior (only CircularGauge, not SemiCircularGauge) and timing with AndroidX upgrade confirm this is a user-side configuration issue. The issue is 4+ years old with no comments.

actions.description: Explain that OnSizeChanged has never been [Obsolete] and suggest checking class hierarchy

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for the report. We investigated this and can confirm that `SKCanvasView.OnSizeChanged` has **never** been marked `[Obsolete]` in any SkiaSharp version, including 2.80.2.

We inspected the compiled `SkiaSharp.Views.Android.dll` from the 2.80.2 NuGet package using reflection metadata — `OnSizeChanged` has no `[Obsolete]` attribute. The only `[Obsolete]` method in `SKCanvasView` is `OnDraw(SKSurface, SKImageInfo)` (which was replaced by `OnPaintSurface`). We also built a .NET Android library that inherits from `SKCanvasView` and overrides `OnSizeChanged` with the latest SkiaSharp — zero warnings.

The fact that only your `CircularGauge` class shows this warning (while `SemiCircularGauge` does not) strongly suggests the issue is in the `CircularGauge` class hierarchy rather than in SkiaSharp. Some things to check:

1. **Intermediate base class** — Does `CircularGauge` inherit directly from `SKCanvasView`, or from an intermediate class that might mark `OnSizeChanged` as `[Obsolete]`?
2. **Clean rebuild** — Delete `bin`/`obj` folders after the Xamarin.AndroidX upgrade. Stale build artifacts can produce phantom warnings.
3. **AndroidX binding metadata** — The Xamarin.AndroidX packages sometimes add `[Obsolete]` through binding metadata files. Check if any `.xml` transform files in your project affect the inheritance chain.

**Tested versions:**
| Version | Result |
|---------|--------|
| 2.80.2 (assembly inspection) | No `[Obsolete]` on `OnSizeChanged` |
| 3.119.1 (latest) | No warning when overriding `OnSizeChanged` |

missingInfo: Full class declaration of CircularGauge (inheritance chain)
Full class declaration of SemiCircularGauge for comparison
Any intermediate base classes between CircularGauge and SKCanvasView
